banner.development: This project is in active development. Versions 0.x may be unstable.

hero.title: Kirkouski Typographic
hero.subtitle: Em dashes, curly quotes, Polish ogonki (ą ć ę ł ń ó ś ź ż), and 80+ typographic symbols — all on AltGr.

legend.deadKeyTip: Press dead key, then a letter to produce an accented character (e.g. ´ then a → á)

windows.title: Fewer input languages
windows.problem: On Windows, every keyboard layout is tied to an input language. To type Polish characters you have to add Polish as an input language; to type Russian you add Russian. The Win+Space switcher then cycles through everything you've added — and the more languages, the worse the toggle.
windows.case1: {en} + Polish typing = 2 input languages with the standard Polish layout. With US+POL, just {en} (1 input language).
windows.case2: {en} + {ru} + Polish typing = 3 input languages with the standard Polish layout. With US+POL, only {en} + {ru} (2 input languages).
windows.solution: The US+POL Typographic layout registers under English (US) instead of Polish — Polish characters live on AltGr, English stays your system language, and the Win+Space cycle stays short.

typography.title: Typography on the keyboard
typography.lead: Real typographic characters at your fingertips, not Unicode-picker excavations:
typography.symbols: Euro, pound, copyright, arrows, math symbols — all on AltGr
typography.note: No compose key, no autocorrect rule to maintain, no copy-pasting from Character Map.

download.title: Download
download.subtitle: Get the layout for your system

windows.step1_exe: Download and run the .exe installer — it handles everything automatically and prompts for a reboot
windows.step1_zip: Or download the .zip for manual install: extract, right-click install.ps1 and select "Run with PowerShell"
windows.step2: RESTART YOUR COMPUTER before using the new layout. The installer will prompt you. Don't switch to the Kirkouski Typographic layout in Win+Space until after the reboot — Explorer will crash if the DLL isn't loaded yet.
windows.step3: After the reboot, go to Settings > Time & Language > Language & Region > Keyboard and add the new layout
windows.loginScreen: To apply to login screen: Win+R > intl.cpl > Administrative > Copy settings > check both boxes
windows.uninstall: To uninstall: open Settings > Apps > Installed Apps, find Kirkouski Typographic, and click Uninstall. For ZIP installs, run install.ps1 again — it detects the install and offers removal. Reboot required after either method.
windows.hardCleanup: If Explorer crashes after a reboot (rare): run {cmd} to remove all traces.

macos.step1_install: Download the .zip, unzip, and move Kirkouski Typographic.bundle to {userPath} (current user) or {systemPath} (all users)
macos.step2_xattr: Clear the quarantine xattr — without this, macOS may silently refuse to load the bundle: {cmd}
macos.step3_logout: Log out and log back in (a full logout, not just the lock screen)
macos.step4_pick: Open System Settings > Keyboard > Input Sources > + and add the Polish/Russian Kirkouski Typographic layouts

about.title: Based on
about.baseline: Built on Polish Programmers QWERTY and Russian ЙЦУКЕН — same symbols in the same positions across all variants.
about.license: Open source under the MIT License.
about.starHint: If you find this useful, a star helps others discover it

items.q: How do I type an em dash on a Polish keyboard in Windows?
items.a: Press AltGr + - (minus key). The Kirkouski Typographic layout puts em dash (—), en dash (–), and other typographic symbols directly on AltGr — no Alt codes needed.

items.q: How do I type Polish characters on a US keyboard without switching layouts?
items.a: Install the US+POL Typographic variant. It registers under English (US) so Win+Space stays clean — Polish ogonki (ą ć ę ł ń ó ś ź ż) are on AltGr.

items.q: Does this work with Polish Programmers QWERTY?
items.a: Yes. The Polish variant is built on the standard Polish Programmers QWERTY layout — all base and shift keys are identical. Typographic symbols are added on AltGr and Shift+AltGr layers only.

items.q: Will this add a new input language to Win+Space?
items.a: The US+POL variant does not — it registers under English (US). The standard Polish and Russian variants do add their respective input language, as Windows requires.
Yes. Download the .bundle, place it in ~/Library/Keyboard Layouts/, clear the quarantine xattr, and log out/in. macOS keyboard layouts are not tied to system language.

items.q: What is the difference between this and Birman's Typography Layout?
items.a: This layout adds Polish diacritics (ą ć ę ł ń ó ś ź ż) on AltGr and repositions some symbols for a more consistent Polish/Russian experience. The typographic symbol positions are compatible with Birman's layout.
